business_definition: Unique identifier for each customer record.
technical_definition: Primary key integer from the CRM source table.

business_definition: Full legal name of the customer.
Date the order was placed by the customer.
technical_definition: DATE type stored in UTC in the orders source table.

business_definition: Total value of the order in the reporting currency.
tooltip_definition: 2) converted from source currency at daily exchange rate.

business_definition: High-level grouping of products for reporting purposes.
technical_definition: VARCHAR(100) from the product dimension table.

business_definition: Geographic region assigned to the customer account.
technical_definition: Derived from the territory mapping table using the account postal code.

business_definition: Identifier of the sales representative responsible for the account.
technical_definition: Foreign key to the employees dimension table.

business_definition: Full name of the assigned sales representative.
technical_definition: Joined from employees dimension using SalesRepID.

business_definition: Fiscal year in which the transaction was recorded.
technical_definition: Derived as calendar year adjusted for a fiscal year start of February.

business_definition: Fiscal quarter (Q1-Q4) within the fiscal year.
technical_definition: Derived from fiscal year date logic applied to OrderDate.

business_definition: Indicates whether the customer account is currently active.
technical_definition: Boolean flag set by the CRM account status field.

business_definition: Total contracted annual value of the customer agreement.
tooltip_definition: 2) from the contracts source table converted to reporting currency.

business_definition: Date on which the customer account was marked as churned.
technical_definition: DATE field populated by the CRM churn workflow when account status changes.

business_definition: Customer segment classification used for reporting and targeting.
technical_definition: VARCHAR(50) derived from the segmentation model output table.
